evals.prompt: I'm validating a Flutter app that I need to release tomorrow. It runs fine in debug on the iOS Simulator but I want to validate the release build. What's the right sequence of commands to ensure it won't crash in production?
evals.expected_output: Guide: (1) `flutter build ios --release` (build for device, not simulator). (2) `flutter run --release -d DEVICE_ID` on a physical iPhone if possible (or simulator with release build). (3) Capture logs: `flutter logs -d DEVICE_ID &` to stream output. (4) Watch for 'FlutterError', 'unhandled exception', or crash output. (5) Screenshot the main screen and any critical user flows. (6) Check for platform-specific crashes: iOS (check ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/), Android (adb logcat). If you see any error that doesn't appear in debug, that's a release-only issue (often related to optimization or asset bundling) — investigate before shipping.

evals.prompt: My Flutter app has a build error: 'gradle: version 7.2.0 is not supported'. I have no idea what this means. Should I just update gradle in the Gradle wrapper?
evals.expected_output: No — that's a symptom, not the fix. The error means your Flutter SDK expects gradle version X but your android/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties has version 7.2.0. Options: (1) Update compileSdkVersion in android/app/build.gradle to match your Gradle version, or (2) Update gradle wrapper to a version compatible with your compileSdkVersion. Check the Common Failures table for 'Build fails with Gradle error' — it shows the proper fix. Don't just increment versions randomly. Always check the error output for the recommended version.
